topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The phone landed on the kitchen counter with a sharp, startling clatter. I stared at the screen, the profile picture a face I didn't recognize, the feed filled with curated perfection that felt like a deliberate insult. My jaw tightened until my molars ached. I had spent the last hour meticulously searching through the password manager, a frantic, jerky motion betraying the rising tide of frustration. When I finally cracked the login, the content confirmed my worst suspicions: Maya, my fifteen-year-old, was cultivating an entirely separate, polished persona online. I snatched the device, my knuckles white, and the silence of the house seemed to amplify the frantic drumming in my chest.

[story 2]

"Show me." The words were clipped, delivered in a low register that vibrated with suppressed tension. Liam’s shoulders were hunched, his gaze fixed somewhere just beyond my shoulder, avoiding eye contact like a startled deer. Every breath he took seemed shallow, hitched. When he finally slid his phone across the table, the screen illuminated a stream of carefully filtered images and cryptic captions. A deep furrow settled between his brows, a physical manifestation of his internal turmoil. I didn't move, just watched, the tightness in my chest escalating with every scroll through this hidden world.

[story 3]

I felt a hot flush creeping up my neck. Every time I looked at the notifications popping up on the tablet, a knot formed deep in my stomach, constricting my diaphragm. It was a betrayal, a clandestine operation happening under my roof. I slammed the laptop shut, the sound echoing loudly in the quiet room. "What is this?" My voice came out sharper than intended, laced with an unmistakable edge of exasperation. I paced three steps toward the living room, my hands balling into fists at my sides, the sheer audacity of the secret making my blood pressure spike.
